bead.title: agent: create project-root candidate refs with retention policy
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Candidate commits created in a temp worktree must be resolvable from the project repository before checks, review, repair, landing, or crash recovery can rely on them. Today the worker returns a raw result_rev from the temp worktree, but no dedicated project-root candidate ref lifecycle exists for the FEAT-010 candidate-cycle pipeline.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:32-103 defines ExecuteBeadResult with base_rev/result_rev/preserve_ref fields but no candidate_ref or cycle_index metadata.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:397-410 in FEAT-010's sequence says candidate refs must be pinned before candidate checks and read-only review, but the loop currently goes from implementation result toward checks/review/land without a project-root candidate ref record.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:696-708 creates land-time preserve refs under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>, but that only covers failed/deferred landing; it is not the per-cycle candidate ref required before checks/review.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:333-365 defines each cycle as recording cycle_index, attempt_id, base_rev, result_rev, and a candidate ref under refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index>.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add CandidateRefStore or equivalent agent package helper that pins each close-eligible candidate commit in the project repo under refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index> before candidate checks and read-only review run.
- Use cycle_index starting at 0 for the first implementation cycle in an attempt and increment for each append-only repair cycle.
- Record candidate_ref and cycle_index alongside existing attempt metadata. The concrete storage target is ExecuteBeadResult/result.json (add JSON fields) plus the append-only bead event emitted for the candidate-cycle step; include base_rev and result_rev so the cycle can be reconstructed after worktree cleanup.
- Retention policy: landed temporary candidate refs may be deleted only after result.json and the bead event/landed evidence containing candidate_ref, base_rev, result_rev, attempt_id, and cycle_index are durable in the project branch. Preserved/manual/parked/conflicted candidates retain the candidate ref for operator inspection and recovery.
- Ensure git -C <projectRoot> rev-parse <candidate_ref> resolves even after the temp worktree is removed.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not move review or repair into the worktree yet.
- Do not implement stale ref pruning beyond the retention behavior above.
- Do not rename existing land-time preserve refs in execute_bead_land.go except where tests need to distinguish preserve_ref from candidate_ref.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-31e07fdc. Depends on ddx-49d8d6cb for coordinator interfaces.
bead.acceptance:
1. CandidateRefStore or equivalent pins candidate commits under refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index> before candidate checks and read-only review run.
2. TestCandidateRefStore_ProjectRootReachable verifies git -C <projectRoot> rev-parse <candidate_ref> succeeds for a candidate produced in a temp worktree, including after that temp worktree is removed.
3. TestCandidateRefStore_MetadataRecorded verifies result.json / ExecuteBeadResult includes candidate_ref and cycle_index plus existing base_rev, result_rev, and attempt_id for the cycle.
4. TestCandidateRefStore_EventRecorded verifies the bead event emitted for the candidate-cycle step includes candidate_ref, cycle_index, base_rev, result_rev, and attempt_id.
5. TestCandidateRefStore_RetentionPolicy verifies approved-landed temporary refs are cleaned only after candidate metadata is durable, while preserved/manual/parked/conflicted candidates retain refs.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestCandidateRefStore" green.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

events.body: {"detail":"Three gaps prevent standalone execution without consulting FEAT-010. (1) Ref namespace is undefined: description uses refs/ddx/candidates/\u003cbead-id\u003e/\u003ccycle-or-attempt\u003e while AC item 1 uses \u003ccycle\u003e, and the 'or the exact namespace defined by the spec bead' hedge defers the authoritative form to FEAT-010 — choosing one over the other is a product-semantic decision that cannot be made safely here. (2) 'attempt bundle/run record' is undefined: no file path, struct name, or schema is given for where base_rev/candidate_rev/candidate_ref/cycle_index are persisted; an agent executing this bead cannot pick a concrete storage location without either inventing one or reading FEAT-010. (3) Retention policy is deferred to spec without inlining the required behavior: AC item 4 says 'artifact metadata is durable' without defining what durable means operationally (which file, which field, what write barrier). All three require FEAT-010 content to be inlined into the description before a sub-agent can execute, and doing so would require reading that spec — which is outside the scope of a safe rewrite.","intake_outcome":"ambiguous_needs_human"}

{"rationale":"All 8 rubric criteria satisfied. Title is imperative and names both subsystem (agent) and change. Description supplies PROBLEM, ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE (three concrete locations with line ranges), PROPOSED FIX, and NON-SCOPE. AC is numbered, each item is verifiable, four named Test* functions are present (TestCandidateRefStore_ProjectRootReachable, _MetadataRecorded, _EventRecorded, _RetentionPolicy), and the final two items are the broad `go test` green gate and `lefthook run pre-commit passes`. Labels carry phase:2, area:agent, area:git, kind:feature, adr:024, and spec:FEAT-010. Parent and dep are both explicit with dep context provided. No /tmp paths or chat references appear. A competent agent given only the bead body can identify which file to create, which structs to extend (ExecuteBeadResult, result.json), which ref namespace to write (refs/ddx/iterations/\u003cattempt-id\u003e/\u003ccycle-index\u003e), and which tests to run.","score":8,"suggested_fixes":[],"waivers_applied":[]}
